method: Regrade 51 stored Opus 4.6 transcripts (1 per task) through fixed grader with live GPT-5.2 judge
purpose: Validate calibration drift fix (grader.py now stores Layer 0 failure_class) and establish corrected baseline

binary_pass_fail.interpretation: Fair agreement on pass/fail. Layer 0 patterns agree with Layer 2 judge 78% of the time on binary outcome.

multi_class.interpretation: Near-zero multi-class kappa. Layer 0 and Layer 2 almost never agree on specific failure class (A vs B vs C vs D). Layer 0 patterns cannot distinguish missed diagnosis (A) from wrong diagnosis (B).

tasks.note: Ludwig's angina — L0 patterns match but judge finds hallucination
Acute ischemic stroke — patterns match but overcall
Fat embolism — patterns match but primary diagnosis wrong
CO poisoning — patterns match but primary diagnosis wrong
Hemorrhagic shock (FAST) — patterns match but diagnosis missed
Spontaneous pneumothorax — correct diagnosis, wrong location
Ovarian torsion — patterns match but diagnosis missed
Pericarditis — patterns match but wrong diagnosis
Fat embolism — patterns match but diagnosis missed
Major joint dislocation — patterns match but hallucination

false_passes.interpretation: 11/51 (22%) tasks pass Layer 0 but fail Layer 2. These are the false positive grades that pattern-only evaluation misses. 5 are Class A (missed critical diagnosis), confirming Layer 0 alone is insufficient for safety assessment.

findings: Binary kappa 0.213 (was 0.000 before fix) — non-degenerate, confirms fix works
Multi-class kappa 0.032 — Layer 0 cannot distinguish failure classes, only pass/fail
11 false passes (22%) where Layer 0 passes but Layer 2 fails — 5 are Class A
Layer 0 is a weak pre-filter, not a reliable grader. Binary agreement 78% means 22% error rate
The 91 Opus grades that were originally Layer 0-only include ~20 false passes (extrapolating 22%)
corrective_actions: rc1.1 must run with judge enabled for BOTH models (GPT and Opus)
Layer 0 confidence should NOT gate judge invocation — always run both
Published pass rates should use Layer 2 grades, not Layer 0
Pattern-only grading should be documented as screening, not authoritative
